Ashok Mahto gang.description: Criminal gang active in Bihar, India, led by Ashok Mahto and Pintu Mahto. Responsible for the 2005 killing of MP Rajo Singh and numerous caste-related murders in the late 1990s. Engaged in a long-running rivalry with the Akhilesh Singh gang over control of stone-crushing and sand-lifting operations. The gang was supported by backward castes and targeted upper-caste Bhumihars.

descriptions: The two gangs were engaged in a violent rivalry over caste conflicts and control of local resources.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivalry between Mahto and gangster Akhilesh Singh affected over 100 villages in the Nawada , Nalanda and Sheikhpura districts of Bihar. Between 1998 and 2006, the rivalry, which claimed over 200 lives in Nawada district, stemmed from caste conflict between Bhumihars and Koeris.

Società foggiana.description: The Società foggiana is a mafia-type criminal cartel operating in Foggia and its province in Italy, with infiltrations in other regions. It emerged in the early 1980s as a splinter of Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata and gained recognition as an independent mafia in 1994. Known for its brutality, it lacks a single leader but is organized into family-based 'batteries' with a federal structure. It is considered one of the most violent Italian mafias.

'Ndrina Morabito.description: The Morabito 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Africo, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1950s by Giuseppe Morabito, it is involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, arms trafficking, extortion, corruption, and murder. The clan operates in northern Italy and abroad, with strong ties to other 'ndrine and international criminal networks. Its leader Giuseppe Morabito was arrested in 2004.

Beltrán-Leyva Organization.description: Mexican drug cartel and organized crime syndicate founded in 1996 by the five Beltrán Leyva brothers. Originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel, it became independent and controlled drug trafficking corridors, engaging in cocaine, heroin, and marijuana trafficking, as well as human smuggling, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. The cartel infiltrated Mexican government agencies and Interpol. It was declared disbanded in 2011 after the arrest of its last significant leader, though remnants continued until 2017.

DarkMarket.description: DarkMarket was an English-language cybercrime forum created by Renukanth Subramaniam in London. It facilitated the buying and selling of stolen identities and credit card data, with 2,500 users at its peak. The forum was shut down in 2008 after an FBI infiltration led to over 60 arrests worldwide. A separate darknet market using the same name was shut down by German authorities in 2021.
